sentences.sentence: The first students to study alternative medicine at university level in Australia began their four-year, full-time course at the University of Technology, Sydney, in early 1994.
sentences.translation: 第一批在澳大利亚大学阶段学习替代医学的学生于1994年初在悉尼科技大学开始四年制全日制课程。

structure.text: The first students to study alternative medicine at university level in Australia
to study alternative medicine at university level in Australia
began their four-year, full-time course
at the University of Technology, Sydney, in early 1994

difficult_points: to study 不定式修饰 students，说明他们是第一批什么学生。
four-year, full-time 是并列复合形容词。

sentences.sentence: Their course covered, among other therapies, acupuncture.
sentences.translation: 他们的课程除其他疗法外，还包括针灸。

structure.text: Their course
covered
among other therapies
acupuncture

difficult_points: among other therapies 是插入语，表示课程还包括其他疗法。
covered 的宾语是 acupuncture。

sentences.sentence: The theory they learnt is based on the traditional Chinese explanation of this ancient healing art: that it can regulate the flow of "Qi" or energy through pathways in the body.
sentences.translation: 他们所学的理论基于中国传统对这种古老疗法的解释：针灸能够调节“气”或能量在体内通道中的流动。

structure.text: The theory they learnt
they learnt
is based on the traditional Chinese explanation of this ancient healing art
that it can regulate the flow of "Qi" or energy through pathways in the body

difficult_points: they learnt 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 theory。
冒号后的 that 从句解释传统理论内容。

sentences.sentence: This course reflects how far some alternative therapies have come in their struggle for acceptance by the medical establishment.
sentences.translation: 这门课程反映出，一些替代疗法在争取医学界认可方面已经走了多远。

structure.text: This course
reflects
how far some alternative therapies have come in their struggle for acceptance by the medical establishment

difficult_points: how far 引导宾语从句，询问发展程度。
for acceptance by... 表示争取被医学界接受。

sentences.sentence: Australia has been unusual in the Western world in having a very conservative attitude to natural or alternative therapies, according to Dr Paul Laver, a lecturer in Public Health at the University of Sydney.
sentences.translation: 悉尼大学公共卫生讲师保罗·拉弗博士说，在西方世界中，澳大利亚对自然疗法或替代疗法持非常保守的态度，颇为不同寻常。

structure.text: Australia has been unusual in the Western world
in having a very conservative attitude to natural or alternative therapies
according to Dr Paul Laver, a lecturer in Public Health at the University of Sydney

difficult_points: in having 说明 unusual 的具体方面。
according to 短语引出观点来源及身份。

sentences.sentence: "We've had a tradition of doctors being fairly powerful and I guess they are pretty loath to allow any pretenders to their position to come into it." In many other industrialized countries, orthodox and alternative medicines have worked "hand in glove" for years.
sentences.translation: “我们一直有医生相当有权势的传统，我想他们很不愿让任何觊觎其地位的人进入这个领域。”在许多其他工业化国家，正规医学和替代医学多年来一直密切合作。

structure.text: We've had a tradition of doctors being fairly powerful
they are pretty loath to allow any pretenders to their position to come into it
In many other industrialized countries, orthodox and alternative medicines have worked "hand in glove" for years

difficult_points: doctors being powerful 是 tradition of 的动名词复合结构。
allow + 宾语 + to do 表示不愿让竞争者进入；hand in glove 是密切合作的习语。

sentences.sentence: In Europe, only orthodox doctors can prescribe herbal medicine.
sentences.translation: 在欧洲，只有正规医生可以开具草药。

structure.text: In Europe
only orthodox doctors
can prescribe herbal medicine

difficult_points: only 限定主语 orthodox doctors。
can prescribe 表示法律或资格允许。

sentences.sentence: In Germany, plant remedies account for 10% of the national turnover of pharmaceuticals.
sentences.translation: 在德国，植物药占药品全国营业额的10%。

structure.text: In Germany
plant remedies
account for 10% of the national turnover of pharmaceuticals

difficult_points: account for 表示占比。
turnover 指营业额，不是药品数量。

sentences.sentence: Americans made more visits to alternative therapists than to orthodox doctors in 1990, and each year they spend about $US 12 billion on the therapies that have not been scientifically tested.
sentences.translation: 1990年，美国人去替代疗法治疗师处就诊的次数多于去正规医生处；他们每年还在未经科学检验的疗法上花费约120亿美元。

structure.text: Americans made more visits to alternative therapists than to orthodox doctors in 1990
and each year they spend about $US 12 billion on the therapies
that have not been scientifically tested

difficult_points: more...than... 比较两类就诊次数。
that 修饰 therapies，说明缺乏科学检验。

sentences.sentence: Disenchantment with orthodox medicine has seen the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily during the past 20 years.
sentences.translation: 对正规医学的失望使澳大利亚替代疗法的受欢迎程度在过去20年稳步上升。

structure.text: Disenchantment with orthodox medicine
has seen
the popularity of alternative therapies in Australia climb steadily during the past 20 years

difficult_points: see + 宾语 + 动词原形表示见证某事发生。
disenchantment with 表示对……失望。

sentences.sentence: In a 1983 national health survey, 1.9% of people said they had contacted a chiropractor, naturopath, osteopath, acupuncturist or herbalist in the two weeks prior to the survey.
sentences.translation: 在1983年全国健康调查中，1.9%的人说自己在调查前两周联系过脊椎按摩师、自然疗法师、整骨师、针灸师或草药师。

structure.text: In a 1983 national health survey
1.9% of people said
they had contacted a chiropractor, naturopath, osteopath, acupuncturist or herbalist in the two weeks prior to the survey

difficult_points: said 后接宾语从句，had contacted 是过去完成时。
prior to 表示调查之前。

sentences.sentence: By 1990, this figure had risen to 2.6% of the population.
sentences.translation: 到1990年，这一数字已上升到人口的2.6%。

structure.text: this figure had risen to 2.6% of the population

difficult_points: By 1990 与过去完成时 had risen 搭配。
this figure 指上一句的1.9%。

sentences.sentence: The 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists reported in the 1990 survey represented about an eighth of the total number of consultations with medically qualified personnel covered by the survey, according to Dr Laver and colleagues writing in the Australian Journal of Public Health in 1993.
sentences.translation: 拉弗博士及其同事在1993年《澳大利亚公共卫生杂志》发表文章称，1990年调查报告的55万次替代疗法就诊约占调查涵盖的合格医务人员就诊总数的八分之一。

structure.text: The 550,000 consultations with alternative therapists reported in the 1990 survey
represented about an eighth of the total number of consultations
with medically qualified personnel covered by the survey
according to Dr Laver and colleagues writing in the Australian Journal of Public Health in 1993

difficult_points: reported in the 1990 survey 修饰 consultations。
covered by the survey 修饰 medically qualified personnel 的就诊，according to 引出来源。

sentences.sentence: "A better educated and less accepting public has become disillusioned with the experts in general and increasingly skeptical about science and empirically based knowledge," they said.
sentences.translation: “受教育程度更高、也不再盲目接受的公众已经对专家整体感到失望，并越来越怀疑科学和基于实证的知识，”他们说。

structure.text: A better educated and less accepting public
has become disillusioned with the experts in general and increasingly skeptical about science and empirically based knowledge
they said

difficult_points: better educated 与 less accepting 是并列形容词。
has become 后接 disillusioned 和 skeptical 两个状态。

sentences.sentence: "The high standing of professionals, including doctors, has been eroded as a consequence."
sentences.translation: “因此，包括医生在内的专业人士的崇高地位已经受到削弱。”

structure.text: The high standing of professionals, including doctors
has been eroded as a consequence
including doctors

difficult_points: has been eroded 是现在完成时被动语态。
including doctors 补充 professionals 的范围。

sentences.sentence: Rather than resisting or criticizing this trend, increasing numbers of Australian doctors, particularly younger ones, are forming group practices with alternative therapists or taking courses themselves, particularly in acupuncture and herbalism.
sentences.translation: 越来越多的澳大利亚医生，尤其是年轻医生，不再抵制或批评这一趋势，而是与替代疗法师组建联合诊所，或亲自学习针灸和草药学。

structure.text: Rather than resisting or criticizing this trend
increasing numbers of Australian doctors, particularly younger ones
are forming group practices with alternative therapists or taking courses themselves
particularly in acupuncture and herbalism

difficult_points: Rather than 后接 resisting 和 criticizing 两个动名词。
forming 与 taking 并列，particularly younger ones 插入说明人群。

sentences.sentence: Part of the incentive was financial, Dr Laver said.
sentences.translation: 拉弗博士说，部分动力来自经济利益。

structure.text: Part of the incentive
was financial
Dr Laver said

difficult_points: Part of 后接单数 incentive，谓语用 was。
financial 是表语，说明动力性质。

sentences.sentence: "The bottom line is that most general practitioners are business people.
sentences.translation: “最重要的一点是，大多数全科医生也是商人。

structure.text: The bottom line
is
that most general practitioners are business people

difficult_points: the bottom line 表示最核心的事实或结论。
that 从句作表语。

sentences.sentence: If they see potential clientele going elsewhere, they might want to be able to offer a similar service."
sentences.translation: 如果他们看到潜在客户流向别处，可能就会希望能够提供类似服务。”

structure.text: If they see potential clientele going elsewhere
they might want to be able to offer a similar service
going elsewhere

difficult_points: see + 宾语 + doing 表示看到客户正在流失。
want to be able to 连续使用两个不定式，表示希望具备能力。

sentences.sentence: In 1993, Dr Laver and his colleagues published a survey of 289 Sydney people who attended eight alternative therapists' practices in Sydney.
sentences.translation: 1993年，拉弗博士及其同事发表了一项调查，涉及289名曾到悉尼八家替代疗法诊所就诊的人。

structure.text: Dr Laver and his colleagues published a survey
of 289 Sydney people who attended eight alternative therapists' practices in Sydney
who attended eight alternative therapists' practices in Sydney

difficult_points: who 修饰 people，说明调查对象经历。
survey of 表示调查涉及的对象。

sentences.sentence: These practices offered a wide range of alternative therapies from 25 therapists.
sentences.translation: 这些诊所由25名治疗师提供广泛的替代疗法。

structure.text: These practices
offered
a wide range of alternative therapies from 25 therapists

difficult_points: a wide range of 表示种类丰富。
from 25 therapists 说明疗法提供者。

sentences.sentence: Those surveyed had experienced chronic illnesses, for which orthodox medicine had been able to provide little relief.
sentences.translation: 受访者都患有慢性疾病，而正规医学几乎无法缓解这些疾病。

structure.text: Those surveyed
had experienced chronic illnesses
for which orthodox medicine had been able to provide little relief

difficult_points: Those surveyed 是过去分词短语，指被调查者。
for which 指 chronic illnesses，介词 for 表示针对。

sentences.sentence: They commented that they liked the holistic approach of their alternative therapists and the friendly, concerned and detailed attention they had received.
sentences.translation: 他们说，喜欢替代疗法师的整体治疗方式，以及自己获得的友善、关切而细致的关注。

structure.text: They commented
that they liked the holistic approach of their alternative therapists and the friendly, concerned and detailed attention they had received
they had received

difficult_points: that 从句中 liked 连接两项宾语。
they had received 修饰 attention，过去完成时强调此前获得。

sentences.sentence: The cold, impersonal manner of orthodox doctors featured in the survey.
sentences.translation: 正规医生冷漠、缺乏人情味的方式在调查中很突出。

structure.text: The cold, impersonal manner of orthodox doctors
featured in the survey

difficult_points: feature 作动词表示显著出现。
cold 与 impersonal 并列描述就医体验。

sentences.sentence: An increasing exodus from their clinics, coupled with this and a number of other relevant surveys carried out in Australia, all pointing to orthodox doctors' inadequacies, have led mainstream doctors themselves to begin to admit they could learn from the personal style of alternative therapists.
sentences.translation: 越来越多人离开正规医生的诊所，加上这项及澳大利亚开展的其他相关调查都指出正规医生的不足，促使主流医生自己开始承认可以向替代疗法师的人性化方式学习。

structure.text: An increasing exodus from their clinics, coupled with this and a number of other relevant surveys carried out in Australia
all pointing to orthodox doctors' inadequacies
have led mainstream doctors themselves to begin to admit
they could learn from the personal style of alternative therapists

difficult_points: coupled with 和 pointing 分别补充复合主语，谓语 have led 与复数主语一致。
lead + 宾语 + to do 表示促使主流医生承认不足。

sentences.sentence: Dr Patrick Store, President of the Royal College of General Practitioners, concurs that orthodox doctors could learn a lot about bedside manner and advising patients on preventative health from alternative therapists.
sentences.translation: 皇家全科医师学院主席帕特里克·斯托尔博士也认为，正规医生可以向替代疗法师学习如何与患者相处，以及如何就预防性健康提供建议。

structure.text: Dr Patrick Store, President of the Royal College of General Practitioners
concurs
that orthodox doctors could learn a lot about bedside manner and advising patients on preventative health from alternative therapists

difficult_points: 同位语说明 Store 的职位。
learn about 后并列 bedside manner 与 advising。

sentences.sentence: According to the Australian Journal of Public Health, 18% of patients visiting alternative therapists do so because they suffer from musculoskeletal complaints; 12% suffer from digestive problems, which is only 1% more than those suffering from emotional problems.
sentences.translation: 据《澳大利亚公共卫生杂志》，前往替代疗法师处就诊的患者中18%患肌肉骨骼问题；12%患消化问题，只比情绪问题患者高1%。

structure.text: According to the Australian Journal of Public Health
18% of patients visiting alternative therapists do so because they suffer from musculoskeletal complaints
12% suffer from digestive problems
which is only 1% more than those suffering from emotional problems

difficult_points: do so 指前面的 visiting alternative therapists。
which 指 digestive problems 的比例，those 代替 patients。

sentences.sentence: Those suffering from respiratory complaints represent 7% of their patients, and candida sufferers represent an equal percentage.
sentences.translation: 患呼吸系统疾病的人占其患者的7%，念珠菌患者也占同样比例。

structure.text: Those suffering from respiratory complaints
represent 7% of their patients
and candida sufferers represent an equal percentage

difficult_points: suffering from 短语修饰 Those。
第二分句省略具体百分比，用 equal percentage 指代7%。

sentences.sentence: Headache sufferers and those complaining of general ill health represent 6% and 5% of patients respectively, and a further 4% see therapists for general health maintenance.
sentences.translation: 头痛患者和抱怨总体健康不佳的人分别占患者的6%和5%，另有4%的人为维持总体健康而就诊。

structure.text: Headache sufferers and those complaining of general ill health
represent 6% and 5% of patients respectively
and a further 4% see therapists for general health maintenance

difficult_points: respectively 按前后顺序对应6%和5%。
for general health maintenance 表示就诊目的。

sentences.sentence: The survey suggested that complementary medicine is probably a better term than alternative medicine.
sentences.translation: 调查表明，互补医学可能比替代医学更合适。

structure.text: The survey suggested
that complementary medicine is probably a better term than alternative medicine

difficult_points: that 从句作 suggested 的宾语。
better term than 比较两个术语的合适程度。

sentences.sentence: Alternative medicine appears to be an adjunct, sought in times of disenchantment when conventional medicine seems not to offer the answer.
sentences.translation: 替代医学似乎是一种辅助方式：当常规医学无法提供答案、令人失望时，人们才会寻求它。

structure.text: Alternative medicine appears to be an adjunct
sought in times of disenchantment
when conventional medicine seems not to offer the answer

difficult_points: appears to be 表示推测，sought 是省略 which is 的过去分词。
when 从句说明人们寻求替代疗法的情境。
